finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_01.md:255, chapters/chapter_01.md:257, chapters/chapter_01.md:261, chapters/chapter_01.md:263, chapters/chapter_01.md:265, chapters/chapter_01.md:271, chapters/chapter_01.md:273
finding_results.notes: Replaced the recycled 'drifted / didn't drift / shot early / shot because he drifted' accusation-denial ladder with a new argument shape. Faye now introduces the specific bounty cost (thirty thousand woolongs) and a fresh tactical complaint (Spike flew through her herding formation rather than abstract 'drifting'). Spike's defense shifts from 'I didn't drift' to 'Your line was already broken' and 'It had a gap' — a different claim about whose positioning failed. Jet connects the lost bounty money directly to fuel and two months of the parking spot they just lost, grounding crew friction in economic stakes absent from the opening. The fallout lines ('Wiggly doesn't pay rent' / 'Nothing pays rent') replace the generic 'Thank you / That wasn't support / It counts' with dialogue that ties Ed's interjection to the crew's shared poverty. No line reuses the opening's accusation-denial pair in near-verbatim form. The two exchanges can no longer be summarized as the same fight with the same wording.

summary: Fixed one finding (chapter_01_f002): the Deimos argument replay at the noodle scene. The recycled 'you drifted / I didn't drift' ladder at line 255 was replaced with a new argument introducing the specific bounty cost (thirty thousand woolongs), a fresh tactical dispute about Spike flying through Faye's herding formation, and Jet connecting the lost money to the parking situation. Character voices preserved: Faye sharp and attacking with profanity under pressure, Spike laconic and minimal, Jet list-making costs under stress. The revision adds new facts, costs, and leverage shifts absent from the opening while preserving crew friction and productive dialogue roughness.
